verdict_reasoning: ATG9A is a multipass transmembrane protein (4 TM helices) primarily resident in post-Golgi compartments (TGN, endosomes, VTCs), but with documented PM trafficking. Cell surface biotinylation reveals ATG9A translocates to the plasma membrane via SNARE-mediated fusion of its vesicles. A baseline ~5% peripheral pool increases dramatically (+156%) upon EGF stimulation. The protein has its own membrane anchors (TM domains) and reaches the outer face through regulated vesicular delivery under activation/stress. This fits dual_localization: canonical intracellular baseline compartment with regulated, inducible surface pool via SNARE-mediated exocytosis.
predicted_key_uncertainty: Extent to which baseline ~5% surface pool represents true steady-state presence vs. transient trafficking intermediate; whether clinically relevant targeting could exploit the inducible surface pool under cell-state conditions
